rxn_class_summary: Electro-oxidative intermolecular 1,2-amino oxygenation of alkenes using sulfonamides and alcohols/acids as N and O sources via nitrogen-centered radicals, with H2 evolution at the cathode.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the deprotonated sulfonamide (N-anion, generated by DBU) to an N-centered radical (NCR); cathodic reduction of protons to evolve H2. DBU appears electroactive and may shuttle electrons (oxidized DBU oxidizes the sulfonamide).

key_intermediates.name: Sulfonamide N-anion (I) after deprotonation by DBU
N-centered radical (NCR, II) from anodic oxidation
C-centered benzylic radical (III) from NCR addition to styrene
Benzylic carbocation (IV) from further anodic oxidation

electron_flow: DBU deprotonates the sulfonamide; the resulting N-anion (or a DBU/sulfonamide complex) is oxidized at the anode to an N-centered radical, which adds anti-Markovnikov to the alkene to give a benzylic C-radical. A second anodic oxidation converts the C-radical to a benzylic cation, which is trapped by the alcohol/carboxylate nucleophile with loss of a proton; cathode reduces 2H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: N-methyl-N-pentyl tosylamide (10) competition substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions with styrene, gave both intermolecular 1,2-amino oxygenation product 10a (15%) and intramolecular Hofmann–Löffler–Freytag cyclization product 10b (41%), supporting an N-centered radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: α-cyclopropylstyrene (11) radical clock
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened adduct 11a (14%) was formed alongside non-opened 11b (6%), supporting radical addition of NCR to the alkene.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, DBU, 2aa and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: 1a shows no oxidation peak up to 2.5 V; DBU catalytic current increases with added 1a, indicating DBU is electroactive and mediates oxidation of 1a; styrene 2aa is electro-redox active but EDG-styrenes give low yields, so direct alkene oxidation is not the initial step.

probe_reagents.name: NMR titration of 1a with DBU
probe_reagents.observation: NMR supports rapid proton transfer between 1a and DBU prior to electrolysis.

probe_reagents.name: Current on/off control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without current, confirming electrochemistry is required.

notes: Authors note an alternative pathway cannot be ruled out in which the 1a/DBU mixture is directly oxidized at the anode to generate the NCR (rather than discrete deprotonation then oxidation of the N-anion). Styrenes with strong electron-donating groups give low yields, consistent with the NCR-addition pathway rather than direct alkene oxidation. HFIP/TFE-type fluorinated alcohol solvents are critical for NCR lifetime; DBU base is essential for high yields.
